title: Favicon — inline SVG favicon in generated page
description: The generated index.html has no favicon. Add one. Two-tier approach: if the user has an avatar set, reuse the already-inlined base64 image as the favicon. If no avatar, generate a minimal SVG favicon from the first letter of config.name, styled with the active theme's accent color. All inlined — no extra files.

features: Update src/generator.js — generatePage() to add a <link rel='icon'> tag in the <head> using one of two strategies:

STRATEGY A — Avatar favicon: if config.avatar is set and inlining succeeds, add <link rel='icon' type='image/png' href='<same-base64-data-uri-as-the-img-src>'>. Reuse the already-computed dataUri — do not read the file a second time.

STRATEGY B — SVG letter favicon: if no avatar (or avatar inlining failed), generate an inline SVG favicon as a data URI. The SVG is 64x64, a rounded square background using the theme's accent color, with the first character of config.name centered in white. Encode it as a data: URI (btoa or Buffer.from().toString('base64')). Add <link rel='icon' type='image/svg+xml' href='data:image/svg+xml;base64,...'>.

SVG template for Strategy B: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 64 64'><rect width='64' height='64' rx='12' fill='<accent-color>'/><text x='32' y='32' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central' font-family='system-ui,sans-serif' font-size='32' font-weight='700' fill='white'><first-char></text></svg>

The accent color used in Strategy B must be extracted from the theme. Add a small helper extractAccentColor(css) in generator.js that reads the --accent-color value from the CSS string via regex: /--accent-color:\s*([^;]+);/. Falls back to '#6366f1' if not found.
Place the <link rel='icon'> tag in <head> immediately after the <meta> tags and before <style>.
Also add <link rel='icon' type='image/png' sizes='32x32'> as a secondary fallback using the same source — some browsers prefer explicit sizes.

notes: Keep it pure Node — Buffer.from(svgString).toString('base64') for the SVG encoding (no btoa, not available in Node). The SVG first-char should be config.name[0].toUpperCase(). HTML-escape is not needed inside the SVG data URI since it's base64-encoded. Do not add a second file read — reuse the dataUri variable already computed for the avatar tag.
